food_pairing.name: Rheingau GG with eisbein
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Riesling Grosses Gewachs with boiled pork knuckle eisbein
food_pairing.description: A powerful Rheingau dry Grosses Gewachs has the mineral structure and racy acidity to cut through the gelatinous richness of eisbein with sauerkraut.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Kabinett with currywurst
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Rheingau Riesling Kabinett with Berlin currywurst
food_pairing.description: Curry-ketchup spice and the snap of grilled sausage meet their match in a low-alcohol Rheingau Kabinett whose sweetness cools the heat and acidity slices the fat.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Spaetlese with Konigsberger Klopse
food_pairing.pairing: Fruity-style Rheingau Riesling Spaetlese with caper-cream meatballs Konigsberger Klopse
food_pairing.description: The Spaetlese's sweetness and acidity balance the tangy caper-and-cream sauce, while its stone fruit lifts the delicate veal meatballs.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Kabinett with weisswurst
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Rheingau Riesling Kabinett with Munich weisswurst and sweet mustard
food_pairing.description: A morning-friendly low-alcohol Kabinett echoes the sweet mustard and refreshes the palate against the delicate veal-and-parsley sausage.

food_pairing.name: Dry Rheingau Riesling with schweinshaxe
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Riesling with Bavarian roast pork knuckle schweinshaxe and crackling
food_pairing.description: Dry Rheingau Riesling's racy acidity cuts the fat of a roast pork knuckle while ripe stone fruit flatters the crackling and apple-sauce side.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Auslese with obatzda
food_pairing.pairing: Fruity-sweet Rheingau Riesling Auslese with the spiced Bavarian cheese spread obatzda
food_pairing.description: Honeyed Auslese sweetness tames the paprika and ripe-cheese funk of obatzda, while piercing Rheingau acidity keeps the pairing fresh.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Riesling Trocken with Spargel
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Riesling with white asparagus Spargel and hollandaise
food_pairing.description: High-acid dry Rheingau Riesling cuts the herbal bitterness of white asparagus and lifts the buttery hollandaise, a German springtime fixture.

food_pairing.name: Assmannshausen Spaetburgunder with sauerbraten
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Spaetburgunder from Assmannshausen with Cologne sauerbraten
food_pairing.description: Smoky red slate, sour cherry and fine tannin in a Hoellenberg Spaetburgunder meet the marinated raisin-and-vinegar pot-roast of the Rhineland sauerbraten.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Spaetburgunder with halve Hahn
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Spaetburgunder with Cologne halve Hahn (rye roll, aged Gouda and mustard)
food_pairing.description: Friendly Rheingau Spaetburgunder with red cherry and fine tannin lifts the aged Gouda and mustard inside the rye-roll Cologne lunch staple.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Riesling Trocken with labskaus
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Riesling with Hamburg labskaus (corned beef, beetroot, herring and fried egg)
food_pairing.description: Racy dry Riesling slices the corned-beef-and-herring richness of the sailor's hash while citrus drive refreshes against beetroot earthiness.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Kabinett with matjes
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Rheingau Riesling Kabinett with Hamburg salt-cured matjes herring
food_pairing.description: Crisp low-alcohol Riesling Kabinett slices through oily salt-cured herring while gentle residual sweetness softens the onion garnish.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Riesling Sekt as Berlin aperitif
food_pairing.pairing: Traditional-method Rheingau Riesling Sekt as Berlin wine-bar aperitif with smoked salmon canapes and charcuterie
food_pairing.description: Bone-dry Rheingau traditional-method Sekt with toasty lees autolysis and bright acid is the German celebration sparkling on Berlin natural-wine lists.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Riesling Sekt for Munich aperitivo
food_pairing.pairing: Traditional-method Rheingau Riesling Sekt as Munich wine-bar aperitif before dinner
food_pairing.description: A bottle-fermented Rheingau Riesling Sekt cleanses the palate before a Munich dinner with bubbles and brisk acid, German alternative to Italian Franciacorta.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau GG with pike-perch (Zander)
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Rheingau Riesling Grosses Gewachs with pan-fried Rhine pike-perch and brown butter
food_pairing.description: Dry Rheingau GG with citrus drive, smoky slate and broad mineral palate matches pan-fried pike-perch and brown butter, the classic Rhine river-fish course.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau TBA with Stilton
food_pairing.pairing: Trockenbeerenauslese with Stilton blue cheese and quince
food_pairing.description: Liqueur-like TBA candied stone fruit and saffron tie together salty Stilton funk and the wine's racy slate acidity.

food_pairing.name: Rheingau Eiswein with tarte Tatin
food_pairing.pairing: Rheingau Riesling Eiswein with apple tarte Tatin
food_pairing.description: Eiswein from frozen Riesling berries meets caramelised apple in the tart with crystalline acidity and candied yellow-fruit concentration.
